description: Rewrite eye_detection.js to use head pose estimation (yaw/pitch from 68-point landmarks) instead of broken eye contour centroid approach. The centroid method always returns ~0.5 ratios because eye contour points outline the eye shape, not the iris position.
steps: Remove the broken calculateEyeGaze function that uses eye contour centroid
Implement calculateHeadPose using nose tip, chin, eye corners, and mouth corners from 68-point landmarks
Use yaw ratio (nose-to-eye-corner distances) and pitch ratio (nose position relative to eye-line and chin) as primary gaze detection
Add face bounding box center check as secondary detection
Keep existing state machine logic (warningShownForCurrentDistraction, gazeAtScreenFrames hysteresis)
Keep warning UI, max 5 warnings, 5-second auto-hide
Keep threshold timing: 2 seconds sustained looking away before warning
Add periodic console.log for debugging yaw/pitch ratios
Run tests to verify no backend breakage
acceptance_criteria: Yaw threshold: < 0.32 or > 0.68 triggers looking away
Pitch threshold: < 0.30 or > 0.65 triggers looking away
Face box center check: normalized x < 0.25 or > 0.75 triggers looking away
State machine and hysteresis logic preserved
